name: Hanauma Bay
tagline: A protected marine sanctuary inside a volcanic crater — the snorkeling is like swimming in an aquarium.
price: $25 non-resident, $5 kama'aina; closed Mondays and Tuesdays
parking: $3 parking; lot fills by 9 AM, arrive early or take the shuttle
hours: Wed–Sun, 6:45 AM – 4:00 PM (gates close at 1:30 PM for last entry)
bestTime: Arrive at 6:30 AM for first entry; Wed or Thu for fewest crowds
kamaaina: $5 entry for Hawaii residents with valid ID
talkStory: You have to watch a short conservation video before entering, which honestly is worth it — it helps you understand what you're swimming with. The inner reef is shallow and calm, perfect for first-time snorkelers and kids. Bring your own gear to skip the overpriced rental line. Go early and leave by 11 AM before the afternoon crowds and wind pick up. The fish here are so used to people they'll swim right up to you.
